---
title: "HPC Integration Test Run"
date: today
params:
  branch: ""
  commit: ""
  rockfish_user: "<yourname>"
  rockfish_host: "<rockfish-host>"
  project_dir: "/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf"
  scratch_dir: "/scratch4/eande106/<yourname>"
format: gfm
engine: knitr
---

# HPC Integration Test Run

**Date:** `r Sys.Date()`
**Branch:** `r params$branch`
**Commit:** `r params$commit`

---

## Checklist

<!-- Check off each step as you complete it. -->

- [ ] Environment setup on Rockfish
- [ ] Generate test VCFs (if needed)
- [ ] Generate HPC strainfiles (if needed)
- [ ] Run test_hpc profile
- [ ] Run test_hpc_variable profile
- [ ] Run test_hpc_three_species profile
- [ ] Run test_hpc_cv_pool profile
- [ ] Collect outputs for all profiles
- [ ] Sync to local machine
- [ ] Run local integration tests (test_hpc)
- [ ] Run local integration tests (test_hpc_variable)
- [ ] Run local integration tests (test_hpc_three_species)
- [ ] Run local integration tests (test_hpc_cv_pool)
- [ ] Clean up scratch work directories

---

## Phase 1: Environment Setup (Rockfish login node)

<!-- Run these on a Rockfish login node before any other steps. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
conda activate /data/eande106/software/conda_envs/nf24_env
export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity
```

Verify Nextflow version is 24.10.x:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
nextflow -version
```

Output:
```
```

---

## Phase 2: Generate Test Input Data (Rockfish interactive node)

<!-- These steps require an interactive session with bcftools via Singularity.
     Only needed when VCF release dates change or strainfiles need updating.
     Skip if data/test/ files are already up to date on the current branch. -->

### Start interactive session

```{bash}
#| eval: false
interact -n 1 -c 1 -a eande106 -m 64G -p <queue-name> -t <minutes>
```

### Set bcftools container path

```{bash}
#| eval: false
SIMG=/vast/eande106/singularity/quay.io-biocontainers-bcftools-1.16--hfe4b78e_1.img
```

### Generate test VCFs

<!-- Only needed for local test profiles run on Rockfish (test, test_variable, etc.).
     HPC profiles use full CaeNDR VCFs directly from /vast/eande106. -->

#### C. elegans VCF (single-species profiles)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

#### Three-species VCFs

```{bash}
#| eval: false
# C. elegans
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ce_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. briggsae
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_cb_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. tropicalis
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ct_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_tropicalis/WI/variation/20250627/vcf/WI.20250627.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

### Generate HPC strainfiles

<!-- Required before running test_hpc* profiles. The strainfile embeds absolute
     Rockfish VCF paths so the pipeline reads from /vast/eande106 directly. -->

#### Single-species strainfile (test_hpc, test_hpc_variable, test_hpc_cv_pool)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

#### Three-species strainfile (test_hpc_three_species)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_tropicalis/WI/variation/20250627/vcf/WI.20250627.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

---

## Phase 3: Run HPC Pipeline Profiles (Rockfish login node)

<!-- Each profile submits SLURM jobs and writes to a named output directory.
     --output_dir prevents same-day collisions between profiles.
     -work-dir must be on /scratch4/ (login node local disk is too small).

     Ensure the conda env and NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR are set (Phase 1)
     before running these commands. -->

### test_hpc (fixed architecture, 1 population)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc
```

Output:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

### test_hpc_variable (variable architecture, 1 population)

<!-- This is the largest profile: 8 nQTL x 4 h2 x 2 reps = 64 traits x 4 modes = 256+ mappings.
     Expect ~30-45 min and ~7 CPU hours. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable
```

Output:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

### test_hpc_three_species (3 species, 9 populations)

<!-- Requires hpc_strains_three_species.txt to be populated (Phase 2). -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_three_species \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species
```

Output:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

### test_hpc_cv_pool (cv pool, no legacy assessment)

<!-- Omit --legacy_assess: the legacy path is incompatible with non-marker causal
     variants produced when cv_maf (0.01) < ms_maf (0.05). -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_cv_pool \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

Output:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

---

## Phase 4: Collect Outputs (Rockfish login node)

<!-- Run on a login node after all SLURM jobs complete.
     --work-dir extracts only the cross-validation files (.fastGWA, .mlma, EIGEN)
     from the scratch work directory — NOT the full contents (which can be 100+ GB).
     Collected files go to tests/integration_data/<profile>/. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_variable --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_three_species --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_cv_pool --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

Verify collected data:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
ls -la tests/integration_data/test_hpc/
ls -la tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/
ls -la tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/
ls -la tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/
```

Output:
```
```

---

## Phase 5: Sync to Local Machine

<!-- Run from your LOCAL terminal (not Rockfish).
     A single rsync syncs everything: db/, assessment TSVs, and cross-validation
     work files for all collected profiles. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:<project-dir>/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/
```

Verify local data:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
ls tests/integration_data/
```

Output:
```
```

---

## Phase 6: Run Integration Tests Locally

<!-- Run from the project root on your local machine.
     Each profile requires specific environment variables.
     TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc is required for all HPC profiles (default is ce.test.200strains). -->

### test_hpc (4 mappings)

```{bash}
#| eval: false
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result: <!-- PASS / FAIL -->
```
```

### test_hpc_variable (260 mappings)

<!-- TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS overrides the default of 4. The variable grid produces
     8 nQTL x 4 h2 x 2 reps = 64 traits x 4 modes. Actual count may differ
     slightly from theoretical 256 (e.g., REML retries). -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=260 \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result: <!-- PASS / FAIL -->
```
```

### test_hpc_three_species (36 mappings, 9 populations)

<!-- TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS lists all 9 population names (3 species x 3 groups).
     If --legacy_assess was used, add TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS="ce.hpc,ce.hpc.popA,ce.hpc.popB,cb.hpc,cb.hpc.popA,cb.hpc.popB,ct.hpc,ct.hpc.popA,ct.hpc.popB" \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result: <!-- PASS / FAIL -->
```
```

### test_hpc_cv_pool (4 mappings, cv_pool)

<!-- No TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT — cv_pool is incompatible with legacy assessment.
     TEST_CV_POOL=true activates the cv_pool-specific test assertions. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result: <!-- PASS / FAIL -->
```
```

---

## Phase 7: Clean Up Scratch (Rockfish)

<!-- Only after verifying that local integration tests pass and data is synced.
     The cross-validation files were already extracted by collect_test_data.sh,
     so deleting the work directories does not lose any test-relevant data. -->

```{bash}
#| eval: false
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

---

## Notes

<!-- Record any issues, unexpected behavior, or deviations from the standard workflow. -->
